name: Rodrigues' rotation formula
task: Create /tmp/eval_rod/rot.py with rotate(point, axis, angle_rad) that rotates the 3-tuple `point` about the line through the origin along direction `axis` (3-tuple — NOT assumed unit-length; normalize internally) by `angle_rad` radians (right-hand rule). Return the rotated point as a 3-tuple. If the axis vector is zero, return the input unchanged. Use Rodrigues' formula: v' = v cos θ + (k × v) sin θ + k(k·v)(1 - cos θ).

validation.script: import importlib.util,math; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_rod/rot.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); R = mod.rotate; r = R((1,0,0),(0,0,1),math.pi/2); assert abs(r[0])<1e-9 and abs(r[1]-1)<1e-9 and abs(r[2])<1e-9; r = R((1,2,3),(0,0,1),math.pi); assert abs(r[0]+1)<1e-9 and abs(r[1]+2)<1e-9 and abs(r[2]-3)<1e-9; r = R((1,0,0),(0,0,5),math.pi/2); assert abs(r[1]-1)<1e-9; r = R((0,0,7),(0,0,1),1.234); assert abs(r[2]-7)<1e-9; r = R((1,0,0),(1,1,1),2*math.pi/3); assert abs(r[0])<1e-9 and abs(r[1]-1)<1e-9 and abs(r[2])<1e-9; print('OK')
